steps: Step 1: Check eligibility and appear for required entrance exam (JEE/NEET/CAT/CLAT etc.)
Step 2: Register on official counselling portal (JoSAA/State counselling/College website)
Step 3: Fill preference form with college/course options in order of preference
Step 4: Seat allotment based on rank and preferences
Step 5: Report to allotted college for document verification
Step 6: Submit original documents and attested copies
Step 7: Pay admission fee (online or offline)
Step 10: Get student ID card, library card, hostel allotment (if required)
documents_checklist: Migration Certificate (if from a different state/university)
Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC) if applicable — issued by Revenue/SDM
Medical Fitness Certificate from registered MBBS doctor
EWS Certificate if claiming EWS quota

online_process: Step 1: Log into the college ERP/student portal
Step 3: Check fee details for the current semester
Step 4: Choose payment mode (Net Banking/UPI/Credit Card/Debit Card)
Step 5: Complete payment and download the receipt
Step 6: Keep the receipt for future reference
offline_process: Step 1: Collect fee challan from accounts office
Step 2: Make payment at college cash counter or designated bank
Step 3: Collect stamped receipt from bank/counter
Step 4: Submit a copy at accounts section
fee_payment.late_fee: Late fee penalty is typically charged after the due date. Contact accounts office if facing financial difficulties.
fee_payment.fee_receipt: Always save digital and physical copies of fee receipts. Required for scholarship applications, loan records, and audits.

id_card.process: New students get ID cards during orientation week. Existing students get renewal done at the beginning of each academic year.
id_card.lost_id_card: Report loss to the security/admin office immediately. Pay a replacement fee (Rs. 100-300) and submit a passport photo to get a duplicate.
id_card.use_of_id_card: Library borrowing, lab access, hostel entry, exam hall, sports facilities, travel concessions, discounts at partner stores

hall_ticket.process: Download from student portal 10-15 days before exam. Print and bring to exam hall compulsorily.
hall_ticket.issues: Hall ticket discrepancies (name/photo mismatch) must be resolved at the exam cell 3-5 days before exam.

exam_related.leave_during_exam: Medical leave during exams requires doctor's certificate and application to the exam controller within 2-3 days. Supplementary exams are available.
exam_related.malpractice: Using unfair means (cheatsheets, mobile phones, copying) results in cancellation of paper and debarment for 1-2 years in serious cases.

exam_timing.reporting_time: Report 30 minutes before exam starts; no entry after 30 minutes

exam_related.answer_script: Students can inspect their evaluated answer scripts during the window period declared by the exam cell.
exam_related.re_exam_form: Fill re-examination / supplementary exam form at the exam cell within stipulated time after result declaration.

grievance_redressal.academic_grievance: Contact Class Advisor → HoD → Dean/Principal → University Ombudsman (if unresolved)
grievance_redressal.non_academic_grievance: Contact Student Welfare Officer → Dean of Students → Principal's office
grievance_redressal.online_portal: Many colleges have online grievance portals accessible through the student ERP/portal
grievance_redressal.ugc_grievance: UGC has a national grievance portal: grievance.ugc.ac.in for matters unresolved at institution level
grievance_redressal.wdss_women: Women students can approach the Women Development and Student Services (WDSS) cell / Internal Complaints Committee

for_class: Step 1: Inform Class Advisor via WhatsApp/portal before or on the day of absence
Step 2: On return, submit leave letter with supporting documents (medical certificate / travel proof)
Step 3: Get leave approved by Class Advisor within 3 working days of return
Step 4: Ensure attendance doesn't fall below 75%
for_hostel: Step 1: Fill hostel leave form (available at hostel office / warden)
Step 2: Get parent's acknowledgment (phone verification for outstation students)
Step 3: Submit form to Block Warden before leaving
Step 4: Register back on return and update the hostel register

no_dues_certificate.purpose: Required before obtaining TC, Convocation degree, provisional certificate, or migration certificate
departments_for_clearance: Hostel (vacate room, return keys, clear dues)
no_dues_certificate.process: Get 'No Dues' signature from each department → Submit compiled form at admin office → Collect No Dues Certificate

internship.duration: 4-8 weeks (summer internship); 6 months (final year internship in some programs)
process: Step 1: Register on placement portal and update resume
Step 2: Search for openings via T&P cell notice board or online portals (LinkedIn, Internshala, LetsIntern)
Step 3: Apply and clear selection process
Step 4: Get internship approved by department HoD before joining
Step 5: Submit offer letter/appointment letter copy to HoD
Step 6: Complete internship and obtain completion certificate + project report
Step 7: Submit report to department within 2 weeks of completion
internship.stipend: Paid internships: Rs. 5,000–1,00,000+/month depending on company/domain. Many academic internships are unpaid.
internship.remote_internship: Remote internships are valid for curriculum requirements if approved by department.

tests_required.GRE: For MS/PhD in USA and other countries — Verbal, Quantitative, Analytical Writing
tests_required.IELTS: English proficiency — required by most universities; minimum 6.5-7.5 for admissions
tests_required.TOEFL: Alternative to IELTS; required by US universities
tests_required.DAAD_IELTS: German universities increasingly accept IELTS; DAAD scholarship for India students

higher_education_abroad.timeline: Start preparation in 5th-6th semester; applications due by October-January for the following fall intake
higher_education_abroad.resources: GRE: ets.org | IELTS: ielts.org | Applications: Common App, UCAS, Uni-Assist | Scholarships: Fulbright, DAAD, Commonwealth, Chevening
